ocr.text: In the context of sustainable resource management, Rio Tinto is committed to minimizing its water usage intensity across its
operations in the mining and metals sector. The company recognizes that efficient water use is critical not only for
environmental sustainability but also for maintaining operational viability in a landscape increasingly challenged by water
supply risks. For instance, the current Water Efficiency Ratio stands at 0.8, indicating that for every cubic meter of water used,
approximately 0.8 units of valuable output are generated. This metric is a focal point in our strategy to enhance performance
and manage resources responsibly. Additionally, the total Fresh Water Usage is reported at 3,500,000m³, underscoring the
significant volume of freshwater utilized across our facilities.
WaterUsageMetrics  Value
TotalWaterWithdrawal  5,000,000m³
WaterIntensity  2.5m³/ton
RecyclingRatio  Percentage  TotalRecycledWater  1,500,000m³
FreshWaterUsage  3,500,000m³  WaterEfficiencyRatio  0.8  WaterFootprint  1,200liters/ton
SurfaceWater  2,000,000m³  Groundwater  1,500,000m³  RainwaterHarvesting  [HIDDEN]
TotalWaterReused  30%  WaterManagementCost  €1,200,000  PollutionIndex  0.1mg/L
WaterConservationPrograms  5initiatives  EmployeeTrainingHours  1,200hours  PublicEngagement  3communityevents
WaterSupplyRisk  Medium  RegulatoryCompliance  100%  InvestmentinTechnology  €500,000
FutureTargets  ReduceUsageby10%  SustainabilityIndex  75/100  WaterQualityMonitoring  MonthlyReports
StakeholderFeedback  QuarterlySurveys  WaterAuditFrequency  [HIDDEN]  Long-termGoals  NetZeroby2030
Partnerships  3NGOs  InnovativeSolutions  NewFiltrationSystems  ClimateResiliencePlan  ActiveImplementation
To further illustrate our commitment, the Water Footprint has been calculated at 1,200 liters per ton of product, highlighting the
necessity for ongoing investment in technology and innovative practices to reduce this figure. FutureTargets aim to refine these
metrics, with initiatives such as Rainwater Harvesting being explored to mitigate our Water Supply Risk. Moreover,
Stakeholder Feedback is instrumental in shaping our water management strategies, and the annual reviews reinforce our resolve
to achieve reductions. Ultimately, our collective efforts are directed towards a sustainable operational model that not only
conserves water but also enhances overall efficiency in line with our corporate responsibility objectives.
